image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Ever wonder why you see lightning before you hear thunder?
Context visual: Light travels at about 299,792 kilometers per second. That’s way faster than sound, which is just 343 meters per second!
Escalation visual: So when lightning strikes, the light reaches you instantly. But sound? It takes its sweet time.
Evidence visual: For every five seconds between the flash and the boom, you're roughly one mile away from the strike.
Payoff visual: So next time you see lightning, count the seconds until you hear thunder. That’s your distance check!
Cta visual: Like what you learned? Hit that follow button for more mind-blowing science facts!
